subject: Biology
title: Osmosis: Water Finds a Way
description: A 1-minute de-risking session on Osmosis: Water Finds a Way.

hook.text: Place a red blood cell in pure water and it swells until it bursts.

buildup.text: Water moves from low solute to high solute through the membrane. That's osmosis.

discovery.text: Plant cells use osmosis to stay rigid. Water fills a central vacuole and pushes outward.

twist.text: Too much salt outside a cell pulls water out, and the cell shrivels like a raisin.

climax.text: Hospital saline drips match blood's salt level so your cells neither burst nor shrink.

punchline.text: Water always moves. Cells must manage the flow.

quiz.question: What happens to a red blood cell placed in pure water?
